pattern: Each node's tilt depends only on its two subtree sums, so one bottom-up pass can return a subtree total while accumulating the tilt contributions on the side.
hints: Tilt at a node compares the total value of everything in its left subtree against everything in its right subtree.
If a recursive call hands back the sum of an entire subtree, you can compute a node's tilt from its two children's returned sums.
Keep a running accumulator that you add each node's |leftSum - rightSum| to, while the recursion itself returns leftSum + rightSum + nodeValue.

edge_inputs.description: empty tree has zero total tilt
negatives so subtree sums can cancel

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes and a mix of signs
